TextENGNew: Waking up in a cheap city hotel was far from what anyone would call comfortable.
It reminded me of those long camping trips back in grade school, when I would count down the remaining days on my fingers, desperate to go home.
...Something about waking up this morning brought those memories flooding back.
...Was it a kind of homesickness, born from the desire to be at my wife's side when she gave birth?
As a contributing member of society, as a public servant with duties to fulfill... it was pathetic.
Those gloomy feelings had faded by the time I finished off a breakfast set that, truth be told, wasn't half bad...
The meeting with the informant that Ooishi had arranged was scheduled for tonight.
I couldn't just lie around doing nothing until then.
The Onigafuchi Defense Alliance had been mounting resistance against the dam on multiple fronts.
changeReason: Organization name differs: original says 'Onigafuchi Guardians' but JPN 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟 is rendered as 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' in the new translation, changing the group's identity.

TextENGNew: Among their efforts was a media PR campaign, which broadly fell into two strategic tracks: the militant approach and the peaceful approach.
The militant approach aimed to keep the police in check by publicizing their heavy-handedness, inhumane treatment, and the brutality of the riot squads.
The peaceful approach emphasized how precious Hinamizawa's natural environment was and how it needed to be protected, seeking support for the anti-dam cause on environmental grounds.
...As part of this latter effort, the village of Hinamizawa had invited prominent zoologists, botanists, and environmental groups to promote the area's natural beauty.
According to reports, in recent years they had even organized free sightseeing excursions under the banner of 'Hinamizawa Nature Watching.'
...I would have liked to take advantage of that, but when I inquired at the village office, I was told that registration had already closed and the next tour date was undecided.
``...I see.@k@vS05/13/101300141. So there won't be another round of applications?@k@vS05/13/101300142. ...That's a shame.``
``Are you with an environmental group?@k@vS05/00/104100002. Or maybe from a magazine?``
``No, I'm just a private individual.``
``A private individual? Here for sightseeing, then?``

``...Something like sightseeing, yes.@k@vS05/13/101300145. I have a hobby of photographing beautiful natural scenery.@k@vS05/13/101300146. I read in a magazine that this area has incredibly rare natural features, so I was quite looking forward to it...``
I knew such an article existed, but I hadn't actually read it myself.
...Would this improvised lie really hold up...?
Just as I was thinking that, the person on the other end burst into hearty laughter.
``Wahaha!@k@vS05/00/104100005. Well then, come on over!@k@vS05/00/104100006. The village is a bit hectic right now, but we welcome you!@k@vS05/00/104100007. Wahahaha!``
``I'd be most grateful. Thank you very much.``

``Say, how are you getting here?@k@vS05/00/104100009. By car?@k@vS05/00/104100010. If so, I'll need your license plate number in advance.@k@vS05/00/104100011. No no, there's no ulterior motive behind it... wahahaha!``
That was probably related to the private checkpoints set up along the road to the village.@k...If I didn't give them my plate number beforehand, I would almost certainly be held up at the gate...
I considered using the car I had borrowed from the prefectural police, but I wasn't sure whether it was wise to give them that plate number.
...If the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance was the kind of organization capable of orchestrating this incident...@k...there was no guarantee they couldn't trace my identity from the plate number.
changeReason: Organization name changed from 'Onigafuchi Guardians' to 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' matching JPN 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟.

TextENGNew: ......Playing it safe, I decided to forgo using the car.
``No... actually, I won't be coming by car.@k@vS05/13/101300149. If there's a bus or something, I'd prefer to take that...``
``We do have a bus.@k@vS05/00/104100013. Though I hear it's going to be discontinued soon.@k@vS05/00/104100014. You can take that one.@k@vS05/00/104100015. It departs from Okinomiya Station.``
``Thank you very much.@k@vS05/13/101300151. I'll look into it.``

``When will you be coming?@k@vS05/00/104100017. Is this your first time in Hinamizawa?@k@vS05/00/104100018. If you've never been, you must be anxious about a lot of things.@k@vS05/00/104100019. Just let me know and I'll send someone to show you around.``
``Oh no... I couldn't impose on you like that.``
``Wahahaha!@k@vS05/00/104100021. Don't worry about it.@k@vS05/00/104100022. It's all part of our PR effort to protect the village's nature~.``
As the checkpoint had made clear... the villagers harbored no small amount of hostility toward outsiders.
So in order to move freely in and out of the village, I either had to be accompanied by someone like Ooishi from the police, or present myself as someone sympathetic to the anti-dam cause.

Expressing interest in the village's precious natural environment aligned with the peaceful PR track the village was pursuing.@k...Having a minder attached to me was somewhat unwelcome, but accepting their hospitality might actually work in my favor.
With the whole village fired up over the dam protests, I might be fortunate enough to hear some unguarded opinions from the residents.@k......There was really no reason to refuse their offer.
``Is that truly all right...?@k@vS05/13/101300154. In that case, that would really... help me out.``
After that, they gave me the bus timetable and told me which bus to catch.@kSomeone would be sent to meet me at the village bus stop.
...After showing me around a few scenic spots to my liking, they would no doubt usher me to something like an anti-dam information center.
Then, over a cup of tea, they would earnestly try to impress their ideology upon me.
Well, that was the whole point behind all their hospitality. The world runs on give and take, after all.
...I couldn't very well go in a suit.
I pulled some casual clothes from my travel bag and changed into something more relaxed.
After that, I spent some time watching local television to settle my nerves, then left the hotel for Okinomiya Station...
...The bus route I'd been told was on the verge of discontinuation certainly didn't look it.
The schedule was ample, and the number of passengers was far from sparse.
It served as the main artery connecting the area to Okinomiya, the only proper town nearby.
The passengers were predominantly elderly folk who didn't look like they could drive and housewives who didn't seem to have licenses.
...Since the whole area was slated to be submerged at the bottom of a reservoir, there would be no point in keeping a bus route.
So the route was being discontinued... but the logic behind it felt forced, somehow.
I couldn't help but sense the government's malice in it -- cutting off the villagers' transportation to pressure them into vacating sooner.
That tracked... according to the documents, negotiations between the Ministry of Construction and the locals had broken down at a very early stage.
...The government had apparently adopted an adversarial stance from the very beginning.
...It was like that fairy tale about the North Wind and the Sun trying to get the traveler to remove his cloak.@k...This kind of harassment was utterly pointless.
changeReason: Opposite meaning: original says 'Even their bullying had meaning to it' but JPN 何の意味もない means 'utterly meaningless/pointless'; new translation corrects this.

TextENGNew: The reason the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance resisted so fiercely... was that the government had been doing equally underhanded things in return.
changeReason: Organization name changed from 'Onigafuchi Guardians' to 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' matching JPN 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟.

TextENGNew: ...I was someone who lived in Tokyo, so a village or two out in the sticks being flooded didn't much register with me.
...But for the people who actually lived there, it was a matter of life and death.

``This route will be discontinued at the end of the current fiscal year. We sincerely thank you for your patronage. We look forward to your continued support of our remaining lines. -- Okinomiya Transportation``
Gazing absently at the discontinuation notice posted inside the bus, I counted how many stops remained until my destination...
Along the way, we passed through that checkpoint without stopping.
They knew when the bus would come through, so they had opened the barricade ahead of time.
......From here on, this was enemy territory.
...At some point, a thin sheen of sweat had broken out across my palms...
``Now arriving at Ukita Waterworks~. Ukita Waterworks~. Is anyone getting off here~?``
I hastily pressed the stop request button as the announcement called out my designated stop.
...With this many passengers and not a single one getting off here... it must be quite a remote spot.
And indeed, the stop was so desolate and run-down that it clearly saw almost no use.
The moment I stepped off, an atmosphere utterly unlike the bus's interior -- raw and oppressive -- struck me head on.
Right now, neither Ooishi nor Hondaya was beside me.
That's right -- I had just stepped alone into the very heart of enemy territory.
They probably assumed I was nothing more than an ordinary tourist, but that was only because I had deceived them.@k...If through some mishap my true identity were exposed......
The chilling words I'd heard at the prefectural police office came back to me -- that in Hinamizawa, someone could be attacked with a knife in broad daylight and no evidence would remain...
...Did I look suspicious at all...?
My clothes were... thoroughly casual.
The perfect outfit for a city dweller who'd come to the countryside looking for a rustic getaway.
A camera and a knapsack.@kInside, a few odds and ends for hiking.
...It was still only June, but perhaps due to unusual weather, the sun already blazed as though it were midsummer.
...Was it suspicious that I hadn't brought a hat...?@k...No... it shouldn't be anything to worry about...
It's fine... I'm just an ordinary tourist.@k...Just ordinary.@kThere's nothing suspicious about me... is there...?
Reassuring myself, I turned back to look at the departing bus--
...and froze. Every passenger by the windows was staring down at me in silence.
They were... gazing down at me... as though they had seen straight through my pathetic little disguise... just staring... looking down.
changeReason: Original says 'proud little outfit' but JPN ちっぽけな自己満足の偽装 (pathetic self-satisfied disguise) implies something more pitiful; new uses 'pathetic little disguise.'

TextENGNew: Through the windows opened to vent the heat, countless stares rained down on me like needles.
......All that rained down were stares.
...No one... uttered a single word.
...But precisely because of that.
...Their silent gazes spoke to me more eloquently than a million words ever could.
......Go back to Tokyo already.@kOutsider.
There was no point in speaking.
...My feeble voice was swallowed by the roar of the engine.
The bus, as if to torment me... pulled away slowly, and only after thoroughly intimidating me did it speed off into the distance......
...A clammy film of sweat rose from every pore of my body.
......According to the files, this was a village hostile toward outsiders.@k...And its population was small.
If they spotted an unfamiliar face, they would immediately identify that person as an outsider.
...On top of that, no one else had gotten off at this stop.
...It was a stop they normally just drove right past.
For someone to deliberately get off here... it must have drawn attention.
...But... I hadn't done anything wrong...
They were the ones who told me to get off at this stop.@k...There was nothing... to worry about.
...I could tell my nerves were getting the better of me -- this was my first time doing anything remotely resembling undercover work.
...My senior colleagues liked to call our generation 'the new breed that never gets nervous'... but that couldn't have been further from the truth.
To steady my breathing... I exhaled deeply, expelling the stale tension from the pit of my stomach.
I couldn't help but pray that my anxiety would dissipate just as the bus's white clouds of exhaust vanished into the air...
There was a small shelter for waiting out the rain, and that was where I was supposed to wait.
Inside the shelter... one entire wall was plastered with anti-dam flyers.
It was as though the villagers' heartfelt cries had been inscribed across every inch of that wall.@k...The interior radiated an indescribable force.
...I wasn't affiliated with the dam project in any way, and yet... I felt a wave of hesitation about stepping inside...
It was a space saturated with the energy of rejection.
And... in that taut, charged space... there sat a girl.
Inside that shelter wallpapered with bold, brush-stroked documents denouncing dam policy and rallying cries for action... the girl sat with drowsy, half-lidded eyes, as if on the verge of sleep.
The image was so incongruous... and yet, somehow dreamlike... that I could only stand there in a daze for a moment.
If I stepped inside, there was no telling what clumsy noises I might make.
...I couldn't disturb this girl's slumber with something like that...
And so I gave up on entering the shelter.
It was just a child sleeping -- so why was I so worked up about it?
...Then, I realized.
This girl was... the very image of the ideal child my wife and I had dreamed of.

Even after multiple examinations at the hospital, we hadn't been able to confirm the baby's sex.@k...Whether we could tell if it was a boy or girl ultimately depended on the angle during the ultrasound.
There was certainly no guarantee it was a girl either... but the chances were said to be high.
Yukie still insisted the odds were fifty-fifty, boy or girl.
...But deep down, I had already decided that our child... was going to be a girl.
From the day I made up my mind until now... imagining what kind of person our child would grow up to be was... so endlessly enjoyable I couldn't help myself.
Among all those imagined forms... the one I had pictured as the most ideal... was embodied in the girl right before my eyes.
...Of course, there was some wishful thinking involved.
...In my imagination, her hair had been on the shorter side.@kBut this girl's hair was long.
It was as though my ideal image was being rewritten to match this girl's appearance... an almost unsettling illusion.@k...But it wasn't an unpleasant one.
...Just gazing at a girl like this... I felt as though I was somehow tainting her.
...She possessed a certain purity... no, a divinity about her.
``Ngh... yaaawwwn.........``
...That angelic face broke into a yawn wide enough to reveal her back teeth, and she abruptly woke up.
...I hadn't meant to be staring.

...But locking eyes with a girl who'd just woken up like this... somehow made me feel as guilty as if I'd been caught doing something mischievous.@kI had nothing to be ashamed of... and yet I became flustered all the same.
The girl spoke.@k...No... chirped?

changeReason: Sound transliteration changed: JPN みぃ is rendered as 'Meep' in original but 'Mii' in new, which is a direct transliteration of the Japanese sound.

TextENGNew: ...Was that English... 'me'?@k As in, 'me,' pointing to herself...?
changeReason: Completely different interpretation: original references a puppet show character; JPN 英語のme？ asks if it's the English word 'me'; new translation matches the Japanese.

TextENGNew: I decided it was just some kind of childish greeting.
...To show I wasn't a suspicious person... I reflexively echoed it back.
The girl... stared at me with a doll-like expression -- cute, yet somehow inorganic... difficult to read.
...Well... I couldn't blame her.
...She'd woken up to find a strange man right in front of her who responded to her 'mii' with a 'mii' of his own.
...Suspicious.@kShe definitely thought I was suspicious.
We both... fell silent.
No, the only one at a loss was me...
...The girl before me stared intently, as if gauging my next move.
Being met with this kind of silence... I felt compelled to be the one to break it.
...Damn it... this girl was far better at interrogation than Ooishi...
``I-- ...I'm not anyone suspicious...@k@vS05/13/101300162. ...I'm...``
The expression I could only have described as cold and unreadable blossomed into a radiant smile, beaming straight at me.
...There's that expression, 'an angelic smile'... and I was convinced it had been coined for this girl alone...
Perhaps her 'nipah~~' smile was her way of asking me to return one of my own...
That was it... this was a child's form of communication.
Having the other person mirror your actions -- a rudimentary way of establishing mutual understanding...
``Ni... nipaaah...☆``
......A warm, gentle... early summer afternoon.
...What... was I... doing?
...Whatever, I didn't care anymore...@kSomehow... this was fun in its own way...
If an angel is someone who can make people happy with nothing but a smile...@k...then this girl was surely one.
...I couldn't help but pray that my own child, soon to be born... would grow up to be like her...
That strange, tender interlude was brought to an end by the arrival of a car.
An old man waved at me from the driver's seat, wearing a sheepish grin.
``Hello there, good afternoon~!@k@vS05/00/104100024. Sorry I'm late!@k@vS05/00/104100025. You're the tourist who called yesterday, yes?``
``Oh-- ah, yes!``

``Sorry to keep you waiting.@k@vS05/00/104100027. Got a bit held up with farm work, you see!@k@vS05/00/104100028. Go ahead and hop right in.@k@vS05/00/104100029. Looks like the weather might turn, so I'll give you the quick tour.``
``...Huh? The weather?``

``It changes fast around here~.@k@vS05/00/104100031. We'll probably get an evening shower again today.@k@vS05/00/104100032. If that happens, you won't be taking any pictures.@k@vS05/00/104100033. Come on now, in you go.``
I turned around to find the girl standing right behind me, her face alight with curiosity.
...Her manner was like a stray kitten that starts following you after you play with it for a bit... utterly endearing.
``Well now... if it isn't Rika-chama!!@k@vS05/00/104100035. What a blessing... what a blessing~...``
...A girl named Rika, beaming with an innocent smile, and an old man clasping his prayer beads in gratitude at the sight of it.@k...It was a peculiar scene.
...Then the girl suddenly turned to face me, tilting her head in a charmingly inquisitive way. Noticing this, the old man realized I was there and introduced me as a tourist visiting Hinamizawa.
``...A young man who has come to see the village.``
The girl -- Rika-chan -- said this with a smile as she took hold of my arm.

``W-well... yeah.@k@vS05/13/101300170. I heard that the natural environment around this village is incredibly precious, so I wanted to capture it with my camera.@k@vS05/13/101300171. ...I may not look it, but I'm actually quite passionate about photography...``
``...Tomitake the Second.``
``...Makino.@k@vS05/27/100500015. May I come along too?``
``N-now now, Rika-chama, there's nothing interesting for you to see!@k@vS05/00/104100039. Why don't you go play with a ball near the shrine grounds!``
An openly displeased expression settled across Rika-chan's face.
Wearing your emotions plainly on your face was a privilege reserved for young children.
``If it wouldn't be any trouble... could you bring this child along with us?``
When I threw her that lifeline, Rika-chan's face broke into that full-beamed 'nipah~~' smile as she clung to me once more.
......Ahh... ...not bad at all...
I want a girl like this... to call me Papa...
...No, wait... Father, Daddy... ...Papa... ...hrnnnngh...☆
...What was I doing?
...Ever since meeting this girl, I'd been completely thrown off my game...@k...Get a grip.

``Can't be helped, I suppose.@k@vS05/00/104100041. Once Rika-chama sets her mind on something, there's no telling her no.@k@vS05/00/104100042. ...Well then, hop on in.@k@vS05/00/104100043. Come now, you too, Rika-chama.``
The old man got out of the driver's seat and opened the sliding door of the van, which had 'Onigafuchi Alliance Town Council' painted on the side.
Rika-chan pushed past me, dove into the seat first, and began bouncing around like a fish.
...She seemed to be enjoying the springy cushions with her whole body.
``Now now, Rika-chama!@k@vS05/00/104100045. Our guest won't be able to sit down if you keep that up!!@k@vS05/00/104100046. Hey, Rika-chama...!!``
......Ahh... ...girls really are wonderful...
I wonder if my child will be this playful too...
No, no, I mustn't!@k That parenting book said you shouldn't lavish blind affection on your child...
Kids are kids... you have to raise them with discipline, not spoil them...
``...It's soft and bouncy, nipah~~~☆``
...I was definitely going to be a hopeless father...@kIt seemed my fate as a doting parent was sealed before my child was even born...
``Well then, sir, I'll take you around to some scenic spots.@k@vS05/00/104100048. Have you had lunch yet?``
``Yes.@k@vS05/13/101300178. I had a light meal at the hotel.``
``Is that so?@k@vS05/00/104100050. Well then, shall we be off?``
``...We're setting off on a Hinamizawa tour.@k@vS05/27/100500019. Yaaay~!``
``Um, is it... all right if I call you Rika-chan?``
``...That's right.@k@vS05/27/100500021. My name is Furude Rika.@k@vS05/27/100500022. ...I can already do addition with carrying and everything.``
``Oh, is that so?@k@vS05/13/101300181. That's... impressive.@k@vS05/13/101300182. Hahaha.``
``...And I don't use my fingers.@k@vS05/27/100500024. I can do it all in my head.``
...The way she insisted on that was almost unbearably cute...
Before I knew it, the tension of being alone in enemy territory had vanished as if it were never there.
...I felt a quiet gratitude for having met this girl.
``All right then, let's get going!!@k@vS05/00/104100052. Hinamizawa Nature Watching!``
The old man stepped enthusiastically on the gas.

...The old man (apparently named Makino.@k...The same Makino listed as the auditor of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance?) showed me around to places that were all breathtakingly beautiful. Even though I wasn't really here for sightseeing, the scenery was stunning enough to take my breath away.
changeReason: Organization name changed from 'Onigafuchi Guardians' to 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' matching JPN 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟.

TextENGNew: To begin with, unlike the monotonous cityscapes I was used to, the charming village scenery -- however unremarkable it might seem to the villagers -- possessed a tranquil warmth.
At first I was only clicking the shutter as part of my tourist act, but before long I found myself taking pictures out of genuine interest.
Tourist spots had their own calculated charm, but this place had none of that.@k...I was especially captivated by its unspoiled innocence.
...If not for the dam controversy... I'd love to bring Yukie here someday.@kI wanted her to breathe this fresh air, to see this dazzling green landscape that seemed to wash your eyes clean.
``...Is something like this really that interesting?@k@vS05/27/100500026. Akasaka, you're a strange person.``

``No, no -- these old cylindrical mailboxes are quite rare nowadays.@k@vS05/13/101300184. There's a certain nostalgic charm to them... don't you think they have a quiet elegance?@k@vS05/13/101300185. Hahahaha.``
``...Are you enjoying Hinamizawa?``

``Hm?@k@vS05/13/101300187. ...Yeah. I'm enjoying it.@k@vS05/13/101300188. Once she's out of the hospital, I'd love to bring my wife here sometime.@k@vS05/13/101300189. ...Along with the child who'll be born by then.``

``Right now she's in the hospital, waiting for the birth.@k@vS05/13/101300191. ...The due date is coming up soon.@k@vS05/13/101300192. ...Haha, it'd be nice if they turn out to be a cute girl like you.``
``Sir, you're about to be a father!@k@vS05/00/104100054. That's no good at all!@k@vS05/00/104100055. Leaving your wife behind to go gallivanting around out here...!``
For a moment, I was at a loss for words.
...Makino was absolutely right.
There was no good reason for a man with his wife about to give birth to be playing tourist in a place like this.@k...Had I made a slip?
``Ah... oh, well!@k@vS05/13/101300195. This trip to Hinamizawa had been planned for a long time...@k@vS05/13/101300196. ...My wife's family is with her, so... it's fine...``

``If you had it planned that far in advance,@k@vS05/00/104100057. how'd you miss the signup for the Nature Watching tour?@k@vS05/00/104100058. If you'd made it, you could've ridden around on a nice tour bus instead of being cramped in here.``
...Makino didn't seem to be probing or fishing for anything in particular.
...But if this conversation continued much longer... I might let something slip without meaning to.
...I cut the discussion short by pretending to take another photograph.
``Well then, for our last stop, let me take you to the place with the best view of all!``
It was just before evening. The sun still beat down harshly, but a faint coolness had begun to thread through the wind.
It seemed my tour of Hinamizawa with Rika-chan was drawing to a close.
``...The place with the best view?``
``Why, Rika-chama, where else could it be but the view from the shrine grounds!``
Hearing that, Rika-chan's smile brightened even further.
``...Then I shall invite Akasaka to my home.``
``Huh?@k@vS05/13/101300198. Rika-chan's home... what do you mean?``
``The shrine.@k@vS05/00/104100062. Furude Shrine.@k@vS05/00/104100063. ...It sits on a hilltop.@k@vS05/00/104100064. You can see some truly beautiful scenery from there!``
changeReason: Original says 'observation platform' but JPN 高台にあります means 'it sits on a hilltop'; new translation accurately describes the location rather than inventing a platform.

TextENGNew: ``...Furude... Shrine.``
A girl who called Furude Shrine her home -- Rika.
...If I recalled correctly, her full name was Furude Rika.
...And Furude was... that's right.@k...One of... the Three Families.
Though the Sonozaki family was said to hold the real power... the Furude house was still one of the old families that commanded authority in the village.
...No, more important than that right now was... Furude Shrine itself.
Ooishi had told me, hadn't he?
...That within the grounds of Furude Shrine lay the offices of the Onigafuchi Defense Alliance.
changeReason: Organization name changed from 'Onigafuchi Guardians' to 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' matching JPN 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟.

TextENGNew: ...And since the shrine grounds were the Furude family's private property, not even the police could enter without due cause.
Furude Shrine... a place even Ooishi couldn't easily set foot in.@kThe Onigafuchi Defense Alliance's headquarters.
changeReason: Organization name changed from 'Onigafuchi Guardians' to 'Onigafuchi Defense Alliance' matching JPN 鬼ヶ淵死守同盟.

TextENGNew: And now, unexpectedly, I had been given the chance to walk right in.@k...I couldn't have asked for better luck.
``I-- I'm looking forward to it.@k@vS05/13/101300201. Please, by all means.``
``All right then, let's hurry!@k@vS05/00/104100066. The weather is starting to look a bit dodgy!``
Before I knew it, the sky had turned overcast.
...It looked like an evening shower could arrive at any moment.
The cicadas, too, as if trying to finish their daily quota of song before the rain, cried out with even greater fervor.
